title: Validate GNSS build/provision epoch floor on physical receivers
description: Hardware validation follow-up from project-LICHEN-xj8k. The no-hardware decision accepts firmware build epoch and optional board provision epoch only as a lower-bound freshness floor, not an authoritative clock source. Validate on physical GNSS-capable targets that cold-start/default GNSS timestamps below the effective epoch floor are rejected for wall-clock establishment, that valid GNSS time at or above the floor is accepted, and that position fixes can remain usable with fix_time_unix_valid=false when time is rejected. Requires physical GNSS hardware and real/controlled GNSS time behavior, so skip in no-hardware loops.
notes:
No-hardware loop block 2026-07-02: acceptance requires unavailable physical hardware or real external endpoints (GNSS receivers/time behavior, battery/PMIC ADC telemetry, physical LCI firmware, real BLE app/phone/GATT policy, or board-level validation). No further software-only work is currently separable inside this Bead; resume with physical devices/endpoints available or split a new no-hardware child if a specific simulator/test-harness gap is discovered.
User input 2026-07-02: build-time stamps in firmware or board/provisioned stamps can be used as the epoch floor for GNSS time freshness. This aligns with the existing design: firmware build epoch and optional board provision epoch are lower-bound freshness floors, not authoritative clocks; physical validation remains blocked on GNSS-capable hardware.
